All are discoverable fro

no_solution_leakage.reason: instruction.md explicitly prescribes the exact solution: specific method names (sessionReadTimeout, longFrom, fetchNodeTimeout), exact field names (nodeTimeoutCache), exact types (ConcurrentMap<URI, Duration>), exact constant values (Duration.ofSeconds(30)), exact class structure (NodeClientKey with URI uri and Duration readTimeout), and exact computation formula (max(sessionReadTimeout, nodeTimeout) + READ_TIMEOUT_BUFFER). This is not describing what's broken — it's dictating what to write.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert on gold-specific implementation details via string matching: exact method signature 'static Duration sessionReadTimeout(Capabilities caps)', exact field name 'nodeTimeoutCache', exact type 'ConcurrentMap<URI, Duration> nodeTimeoutCache', exact class name 'NodeClientKey', exact helper name 'longFrom', exact return type 'Optional<Duration> fetchNodeTimeout'. An alternative correct fix using different names (e.g., 'getPageLoadTimeout', 'NodeHttpKey', 'parseNumericValue') would fail all

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: All 8 fail-to-pass tests are purely text-matching: they read HandleSession.java and assert substrings/regex patterns exist in the source code (e.g., 'assert "READ_TIMEOUT_BUFFER" in content'). Only the pass-to-pass tests (bazel build/test) execute the code. The f2p tests never import, instantiate, or call any Java method.

test_not_tautological.reason: Since f2p tests only check for string presence in source code, an agent could pass all of them by adding dead code: a comment block or unused methods containing the required strings (e.g., 'static Duration sessionReadTimeout(Capabilities caps) { return null; }') without actually fixing the timeout bug. The bazel build test is pass-to-pass, not f2p.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution file exists at /workspace/selenium (accessible via solve.sh path) and the agent could read it. Additionally, since tests are pure string-matching, the agent could grep test_outputs.py for the exact strings being asserted and simply inject them as comments or dead code into HandleSession.java.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All behaviors listed in instruction.md (sessionReadTimeout method, READ_TIMEOUT_BUFFER constant, NodeClientKey class, longFrom helper, fetchNodeTimeout returning Optional, nodeTimeoutCache field, httpClients map type change, effective timeout calculation) are tested, plus compilation and formatting checks.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with a clear header comment, grouped into 'fail_to_pass' and 'pass_to_pass' sections with comments, and each test has a descriptive docstring explaining what it checks and why.

structured_data_schema.reason: The task produces Java source code modifications, not structured data output like JSON/CSV/API/DB.

no_typos.reason: No typos detected in filenames, paths, commands, variable names, or method signatures across instruction.md and test files.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: instruction.md explicitly names the exact file to modify (HandleSession.java) with full path, exact method signatures, exact field names, exact types, and exact constant values. This goes far beyond identifying the buggy module — it prescribes the entire implementation at a line-by-line level.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code-only task modifying a Java source file, not a configuration edit task.
